note: Blockers and majors from the 2026-07-16 L1 sweep (12 journeys, full roster). Minor/polish findings are enumerated in report.md but not duplicated here in full structured form.

findings.title: Rate-limit failures never reach the typed ToolInvocationResult contract
findings.expected: A rate-limited tool call surfaces error_kind: 'rate_limited' in the UI.
findings.got: The per-tool rate limiter returns a hard Err before the typed result block is constructed; ToolErrorKind::RateLimited exists and is unit-tested but is structurally unreachable from this call path.
findings.l2_priority: Trigger the per-tool rate limiter live (rapid repeated Run clicks) and confirm the actual rendered error.

findings.title: Missing/invalid credential (auth) failures bypass the typed contract, and the frontend renders any such failure as the literal string "[object Object]"
findings.expected: An auth failure surfaces error_kind: 'auth' with a readable message.
findings.got: resolve_credential_env_vars short-circuits with a raw AppError before the typed block; useToolRunner.ts's catch does `err instanceof Error` on a plain-object Tauri rejection (never true), so String(err) evaluates to '[object Object]'.
findings.l2_priority: Run a tool with no/rotated credential live; confirm on-screen text is literally unreadable.

findings.title: Settings → Network and Settings → Admin are unreachable in a production build, independent of tier
findings.expected: A builder/team-tier customer can reach network-exposure and admin governance controls their Character file (and plan) entitles them to.
findings.got: Both nav items carry devOnly: true gated on import.meta.env.DEV, which is false in any shipped Tauri build — gate is build-mode, not tier.
findings.l2_priority: CRITICAL: verify against an actual production-mode build, not tauri:dev:test, which likely sets DEV=true and would mask this.

findings.title: No pricing/tier information exists anywhere in-product
findings.expected: A prospect can find and understand the pricing/tier model in-product within a short evaluation session.
findings.got: Settings → Account has zero pricing/plan UI; tier is a build-time env var invisible to the user; gated features simply vanish from the sidebar with no explanation.
findings.l2_priority: Confirm live whether pricing appears via any in-app CTA (e.g. triggered by hitting a gate) that static analysis of Settings alone might miss.

findings.title: No binding kind or prompt input captures brand-voice/style during adoption
findings.l2_priority: Confirm live whether an adopted template's first run is generic vs on-brand.

findings.title: Companion-to-gallery handoff drops a non-technical user into the same jargon-adjacent gallery UI as everyone else
findings.l2_priority: Time-to-first-useful-result for a non-technical user landing here live.

findings.title: Two parallel, non-cross-linked adoption systems (Templates/Generated vs Recipes) create navigational ambiguity for a multi-client user
New triggers/schedules created via adoption appear enabled by default with no observed pre-flight review gate
findings.l2_priority: Confirm live whether a freshly-adopted scheduled persona fires before the user reviews output.

findings.title: No persistent/saved brand-voice profile — reference-context grounding is per-build-session only
findings.l2_priority: Confirm whether returning users must re-paste brand voice each rebuild.

findings.title: Character binding cites src/features/simple-mode/** which does not exist in the repo
findings.l2_priority: Confirm what 'simple mode' actually is live and fix the stale character binding.

findings.title: use_connector auto-fires with no approval card, contradicting the 'routes to me when unsure' bar
findings.l2_priority: Confirm live whether an auto-fired connector call ever surfaces to the user before it's done, or only after.

findings.title: No dedicated citation-tracking/multi-source-verification structure behind companion research — only a prose cite instruction
findings.l2_priority: Run a real multi-source ask and inspect whether citations are real/openable.

findings.title: No locale directive anywhere in the companion prompt pipeline — reply language depends entirely on model inference
findings.l2_priority: Drive a real Spanish companion turn and check for English fallback, especially after a tool-result turn.

findings.title: Companion send-error messages are hardcoded English AppError strings, not routed through i18n
Destructive-action approval/dry-run gate only covers schedule/polling triggers, not webhook/event_listener — exactly the trigger type these characters' JTBDs need it for
findings.l2_priority: Live-confirm no gate control appears on a webhook trigger's detail drawer and that a webhook fire always executes live.

findings.title: Journey doc + two Character files name Monitor tabs 'Console'/'Briefing'; shipped UI labels are 'Timeline'/'Grid'
findings.l2_priority: Confirm live whether a Character can locate the audit/handoff monitor navigating by the doc's vocabulary.

findings.title: Teams Monitor lacks the structural guarantees (typed actor, filter, export) a security-review-grade audit trail needs
findings.l2_priority: HIGH: drive a multi-agent run and have enterprise-admin try to reconstruct who-did-what-when from Monitor alone.

findings.title: Live-slot cap eviction never kills work but is not proactively surfaced — a tile silently vanishes from the grid
findings.l2_priority: Spawn past the cap live and confirm whether the operator notices the eviction in real time.

findings.title: No manual UI exists to link a hand-authored goal to an existing KPI — connection only happens via autonomous derivation
'manual' measure_kind is a standalone-number escape hatch with no cross-check beyond an honest but easy-to-miss label
